title: Jellyflow Model Policy Boundary v1
problem: Jellyflow graph storage currently mixes semantic graph data, canvas layout, persisted editor policy, presentation flags, and XyFlow compatibility vocabulary without a single policy-resolution contract.

evidence.summary: Opened follow-on workstream and froze the task ledger; no code gates run yet.
Added ADR 0002 and a field taxonomy. Chose an additive v1 path: keep persisted fields in Graph, add runtime policy-resolution helpers, and defer schema migration.
Added runtime::policy read-only helpers for node, port, and edge interaction policy. cargo check, policy-filtered nextest, and package clippy passed.

Routed connect/reconnect planners through runtime::policy and added disabled-interaction tests. Delete enforcement remains a follow-on because no delete planner exists. cargo check, rules/runtime-filtered nextest, and package clippy passed.
Updated README and runtime::xyflow docs to point effective interaction policy resolution to runtime::policy. cargo check and runtime-filtered nextest passed.
Closed the lane. Final fmt, workspace nextest, workspace clippy, dependency-boundary, external-smoke, JSON, and diff checks passed.
Reconciled closed-lane follow-on navigation after jellyflow-delete-contract-v1 completed delete selection ownership; schema migration remains a CONTEXT-level follow-on.

continue_policy.notes: This model-policy boundary lane is closed. Delete planner ownership was superseded by jellyflow-delete-contract-v1; persisted schema migration remains a likely follow-on in CONTEXT.md and should open as a separate lane only with schema evidence.
